scenario: You are integrating a model-driven app with an external system. You need to push the data to the external system each time a new contact is created or updated in the model-driven app. You need to ensure that only the necessary columns are used in the plug-in code to improve the plug-in performance. You write the code snippet in the plug-in execute method shown in the exhibit.
q: For each of the following statements about the plug-in code, select Yes if the statement is true. Otherwise, select No.

rows.s: The contactColl object returns the contacts with the same email address.
The contactColl.Count object returns the count of duplicate contacts.
You should use service.Retrieve(contactQuery) instead of service.RetrieveMultiple(contactQuery) to execute the query.
You should use ColumnSet('emailaddress') instead of ColumnSet(true).
You must register the plug-in in the pre-operation stage.
The user must have the System Administrator role to register the plug-in.

why: contactColl returns the contacts matching the query: RetrieveMultiple always returns the record collection based on the query passed to it. contactColl.Count does NOT return the count of duplicates; to get the count use contactColl.Entities.Count, e.g. if(contactColl.Entities.Count > 0) { throw new InvalidPluginExecutionException("Duplicate contact found."); }. You should NOT use service.Retrieve(...) instead of RetrieveMultiple(...): Retrieve returns a single record by its GUID, while RetrieveMultiple returns the collection for a query. You SHOULD use ColumnSet("emailaddress") instead of ColumnSet(true): requesting only the needed column (rather than all columns) improves performance. You do NOT have to register the plug-in in the pre-operation stage (validation/duplicate checks are commonly done in pre-validation/pre-operation, but it is not mandatory for this logic). The user does NOT need the System Administrator role to register a plug-in; the System Customizer role (with the necessary privileges) is sufficient.
